title: ComputationalRegulatoryGenomicsICL/customcage pipeline parameters
description: Schema for the inputs provided with params.yaml

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

maponly.description: Run only the mapping part until bigiwgs or bams

cageronly.description: Run only the CAGEr processing part from bigiwgs or bams

gtf.description: Genome annotation fiel in GTF format

samplesheet.description: Path to the samplesheet file. Mutually exclusive with infolder

infolder.description: Path to the folder with fastq files. Mutually exclusive with samplesheet

sample_name_fields.description: Number of underscore separated fields  denoting sample name when infolder is used

genome_name.description: Name of the reference genome. It is used as meta information

index.description: Specifies a directory with a genome index

seq_platform.description: Sequencing platform used. Required for mapping with STAR

seq_center.description: Name of the sequencing center. Required for mapping with STAR

unique_only.description: Whether only uniquely mapped reads should be considered for downstream analysis.

remove_non_g.description: Whether to keep only those reads that start with G base

params_trimgalore.description: Additional parameters that can be passed to TrimGalore!

cager_sample_file.description: The input CSV samplesheet including the name of the samples, their pairedness status, and the location of bigwig or bam files. Required when cageronly is true.

sourcedir.description: Directory containing either a set of FASTA files, one per reference chromosome, or a 2bit file for the whole reference genome. Used for BSgenome forging

bsgenome.description: BSgenome R package to use (if not forged)

corrplot_tagCountThreshold.description: Threshold above which raw and normalized CTSS are considered for the correlation plot

norm_range_min.description: Defines the lower thresold for fitting the power-law distribution

norm_range_max.description: Defines the upper thresold for fitting the power-law distribution

norm_method.description: Method used for normalizing the samples: powerLaw, simpleTpm, and none are supported

alpha.description: User specified alpha, the `-1 *` fitted slope in the log-log representation of the power-law distribution. If none, the average across samples is calculated and used.

t_norm.description: Total number of CAGE tags in the reference power-law distribution

sample_num_thr.description: Parameters for filtering low expressed CTSS before clustering. `ctss_thr` specifies the lower threshold above which CTSS are considered, and `sample_num_thr` specifies the number of samples where this threshold should be passed.

ctss_thr.description: Parameters for filtering low expressed CTSS before clustering. `ctss_thr` specifies the lower threshold above which CTSS are considered, and `sample_num_thr` specifies the number of samples where this threshold should be passed.

keepSingletonsAbove.description: The tpm threshold above which even a single CTSS is kept during clustering

iq_low.description: Define the lower quantile boundaries of the interquartile range

iq_high.description: Define the upper quantile boundaries of the interquartile range

iqw_tpm_threshold.description: Threshold above which tag clusters are considered for the interquartile width distribution plot

tssregion_up.description: Upstream distance to consider into TSS region for ChIPseeker annotation

tssregion_down.description: Downstream distance to consider into TSS region for ChIPseeker annotation

tsslogo_upstream.description: The number of bases to inlcude upstream of the TSS for TSS logos

consensus_thr.description: Used for defining the consensus clusters. `consensus_thr` specifies the TPM threshold above which tag clusters are considered for consensus clusters, and `consensus_dist` define the maximum distance between the interquartile ranges of tag clusters to be joined together into consensus clusters.

consensus_dist.description: Used for defining the consensus clusters. `consensus_thr` specifies the TPM threshold above which tag clusters are considered for consensus clusters, and `consensus_dist` define the maximum distance between the interquartile ranges of tag clusters to be joined together into consensus clusters.

cfBalanceThreshold.description: Defines the balance threshold above which bidirectionality is considered balanced and enhancers are called

unexpressed.description: Used for selecting only supported enhancers. `unexpressed` is a non inclusive lower TPM boundary for expression when calculating support of enhancers. `minSamples` is a non-inclusive lower boundary for the number of samples where the clusters should show bidirectionality.

minSamples.description: Used for selecting only supported enhancers. `unexpressed` is a non inclusive lower TPM boundary for expression when calculating support of enhancers. `minSamples` is a non-inclusive lower boundary for the number of samples where the clusters should show bidirectionality.

nogtrim.description: Makes the pipeline skip the G-trimming step in preprocessing

bowtie2.description: Switches the aligner from STAR to bowtie2

dist.description: Sets an optical duplicate distance, used together with dedup

save_merged_fastq.description: Whether to save merged fasta files

genome.description: FASTA file containing a reference genome.
